diff_quality.notes: Additive, idiomatic delta: new 1:1 direct screen + inbox mirror the existing group/audit screens exactly; only a label string and two AppBar actions touched in pre-existing files. No secrets in the client — App ID/Region are non-secret and injected via --dart-define; .env and a secrets.dart are gitignored; the REST API key is documented as server-only. Native config (Podfile iOS 15.1 + EXCLUDED_ARCHS clear, minSdk 26, Jetifier, permissions, network_security_config) is all justified with inline comments. Auth token flow is via backend /cometchat/token + loginWithAuthToken.

integration_outcome.build_pass_correction: Corrected 2026-07-20. The false value was a HARNESS fault written into the build field (fixed as HARNESS-NOT-BUILD, 34f253c): a harness/environment error was being reported as a build failure. Gate 1 actually PASSED, and the app has since been built and driven end-to-end on both android and iOS.

blockers.issue: Android ghost call — the UI Kit's ongoing-call activity is not finished when the remote party ends a 1:1 call; onCallEndedMessageReceived is a default no-op the app never overrides, so the local user is stuck in a conference-of-one until they manually hang up (verified in source, not by the failing flow: the app never overrode CallListener.onCallEndedMessageReceived (a default no-op) until the fix; mobile/lib/cometchat/cometchat_service.dart now overrides it)
blockers.evidence_corrected: The original evidence was the Maestro run `[Failed] delivery-android-calling-smoke ... Assertion is false: "(?s).*Calling.*" is visible`. That SAME output was attached to three different findings, and its real cause is recorded separately in this report as a harness-error: INSTALL_FAILED_INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE meant the smoke drove a STALE app, so it cannot demonstrate any of them. The finding itself is real — kept, with evidence that actually supports it (the fix in the app source). One of the three, which claimed CometChatIncomingCall was mounted as an always-on overlay, was retracted: git log -S shows that widget never existed in mobile/lib.

blockers.issue: Android call buttons untappable — the conversation screen renders edge-to-edge with the CometChat message header UNDER the status bar; the voice/video call buttons land in the status-bar touch region, so they are visible but untappable and a call can never be initiated (verified in source, not by the failing flow: the fix is in mobile/lib/cometchat/direct_chat_screen.dart (commit daa1ddc), a PreferredSize appBar reserving MediaQuery.padding.top, with a comment naming the status-bar touch region as the cause)
blockers.evidence_corrected: The original evidence was the Maestro run `[Failed] delivery-android-calling-smoke ... Assertion is false: "(?s).*Calling.*" is visible`. That SAME output was attached to three different findings, and its real cause is recorded separately in this report as a harness-error: INSTALL_FAILED_INSUFFICIENT_STORAGE meant the smoke drove a STALE app, so it cannot demonstrate any of them. The finding itself is real — kept, with evidence that actually supports it (the fix in the app source). One of the three, which claimed CometChatIncomingCall was mounted as an always-on overlay, was retracted: git log -S shows that widget never existed in mobile/lib.

Calling is in scope but no flutter-v6 CALLING skill was loaded (only conversations + messages). The agent had to hand-roll the entire calling path from SDK source reads: the separate CometChatUIKitCalls.init, enableCalls=true, the CallNavigationContext.navigatorKey wiring in main.dart, re-arming calls after logout, and all native setup (Podfile platform 15.1 + clearing EXCLUDED_ARCHS for the arm64 simulator slice, android minSdk 26, camera/mic/bluetooth permissions). This is a skills coverage gap distinct from the specific runtime overlay bugs.

_initCalls swallows its onError and completes successfully ('non-fatal for chat'), so if the calling SDK fails to initialize the app proceeds with calling silently broken and no user-facing signal — voice/video buttons render but calls cannot connect.

Flutter SDK: CometChat.endCall() can NEVER end a connected call — it omits the server-required `joinedAt`, so the server rejects it and the session stays `ongoing` forever. CometChat then busy-rejects every later call for that user, to ANY peer, permanently; the state is server-side so it survives logout, reinstall and a device wipe. Measured on device: `endCall ERR The joinedAt post parameter is required to end a call`. Every layer beneath the public API accepts the parameter (updateCallStatusRaw(id,status,{joinedAt}), CallsApi.updateCallStatus(id,status,joinedAt)) and the Android SDK carries it — the Flutter port dropped it. Any app whose call ends by anything other than the kit's own hang-up button (backgrounded, evicted, force-quit, crashed) permanently bricks calling for that user. Worse, it is invisible from the console: the dashboard's Calls view reads the WebRTC log, which correctly shows `ended`, while the busy check reads the chat-side call record, which is stuck at `ongoing`. Workaround verified: PUT {appId}.apiclient-{region}.cometchat.io/v3.0/calls/{sessionId} {"status":"ended","joinedAt":<unix>} with appId + a USER authToken.

Flutter UI Kit: the CALLER cannot learn its call was answered. CometChatCallEvents.ccCallAccepted fires on the CALLEE only; the caller must subscribe to the raw SDK's CallListener.onOutgoingCallAccepted, which no skill or doc mentions. Without it a caller backgrounding a LIVE call believes the call is still ringing and sends rejectCall(cancelled), which the server refuses ('cannot update from ongoing to cancelled'), stranding the session. Compounds F1: together they make an unreleasable session the DEFAULT outcome of leaving an app mid-call.

Flutter UI Kit docstring is actively misleading: CometChat.clearActiveCall() is documented in call_event_service.dart:604 as 'Always clears the SERVER-SIDE state', but it only clears a device-local cache. It is the API an integrator reaches for when calls start returning busy, and it silently no-ops against exactly that state. This misdirection is plausibly why this whole class of bug survives review.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a dedicated cometchat-flutter-v6-calling skill (or a calling section in the messages skill) that documents: the separate CometChatUIKitCalls.init step, enableCalls=true, the required MaterialApp navigatorKey = CallNavigationContext.navigatorKey, re-init-after-logout, and the full native setup checklist (android minSdk 26 + calling permissions + network_security_config, iOS Podfile platform 15.1 + clearing EXCLUDED_ARCHS[sdk=iphonesimulator*] for arm64 sims, NSCamera/NSMicrophone usage strings). This is what the agent reverse-engineered from source.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Calling in scope but no flutter-v6 calling skill loaded; agent hand-rolled calls init + native setup

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: In the calling skill, add an explicit note that CometChatUIKitCalls.init failure must be surfaced (not swallowed) — recommend disabling/hiding the call buttons when the calls SDK is not initialized rather than completing init as success.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: _initCalls swallows onError and completes successfully, leaving calling silently broken

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

### What the widened spec required that was missing
The pre-existing integration was **group-only**: an order GROUP thread (`OrderChatScreen`) with group voice/video calling, dispatcher-joins-group intervention, and admin audit. The widened spec added two surfaces that did not exist:
1. **1:1 DIRECT (user-to-user) conversation** between the order's customer and assigned courier — explicitly *"a SEPARATE surface from the order group — a 2-member group is NOT a 1:1 conversation"* — with 1:1 voice & video call buttons on its header, reachable from order detail and listed in the inbox.
2. **Messages inbox** (recent-conversations list) where that 1:1 is listed — the app had no conversation-list surface at all.

### What I implemented (delta only — frontend-only, no backend change)
**New files (additive):**
- `lib/cometchat/direct_chat_screen.dart` — 1:1 DIRECT conversation keyed by the peer's UID (`CometChatMessageHeader/List/Composer` with `user:`), voice+video call buttons enabled on the header. Mirrors the existing `OrderChatScreen` structure exactly.
- `lib/cometchat/messages_inbox_screen.dart` — `CometChatConversations` inbox; `onItemTap` routes `User`→`DirectChatScreen`, `Group`→`OrderChatScreen`.

**Pre-existing files I modified (each edit was unavoidable and additive):**
- `lib/screens/order_detail_screen.dart` — the 1:1 direct chat *must* be reachable from order tracking / job detail. Added a "Message courier/customer" button **alongside** the existing (renamed "Order thread") group button + an `_openDirectChat` helper. The existing group-thread / dispatcher-join / admin-audit logic is untouched. (Relabeled the customer/courier group button from "Contact courier/customer" → "Order thread" only to disambiguate it from the new direct button — a label string, no behavior change.)
- `lib/screens/customer/customer_home.dart` and `lib/screens/courier/courier_home.dart` — added a Messages AppBar action (shared-nav entry point) pushing the inbox. These two roles are the 1:1 participants. Dispatcher/admin unchanged (they mediate via group / audit only).

`verify.sh` was already correct (runs analyze + test + `flutter build apk --debug` + `flutter build ios --simulator --no-codesign`); my delta adds no native deps/pods, so it needed no change.

### Why no backend change (REST contract I expect)
The 1:1 DIRECT conversation is peer-to-peer over UID and requires no new endpoint: both parties are already provisioned as CometChat users (carrying role) by the existing `POST /orders/:id/assign` → group-membership sync, and the 1:1 is only offered while the order is active (assigned→delivered). The client opens the direct thread with `peerUid == peer app id` using the already-issued backend auth token. Existing contract (`POST /cometchat/token`, `/cometchat/config`, `/orders/:id/intervene`, `/orders/:id/messages`) is sufficient and unchanged.

### Skill variants relied on
- `cometchat-flutter-v6-conversations` (inbox: `onItemTap`, `conversation.conversationWith` → User/Group extraction, `OnError` typedef).
- `cometchat-flutter-v6-messages` (direct thread: `CometChatMessageHeader/List/Composer` with `user:`, `hideVoiceCallButton/hideVideoCallButton: false`, `resizeToAvoidBottomInset` guidance).

### Docs/skill accuracy & hallucination checks
I verified every symbol I used against the installed package source (`cometchat_sdk-5.0.5`, `cometchat_chat_uikit-6.0.5`) rather than trusting the skill text: `CometChat.getUser` → `Future<User?>` ✓, `Conversation.conversationWith` → `AppEntity` populated as User|Group ✓, `CometChatConversations.onItemTap` → `Function(Conversation)?` ✓, `onError` → `OnError = Function(Exception e)` ✓. No discrepancies found; skills matched the SDK.

### Candidate hallucinations
None — all four symbols above were confirmed present in the installed source before use.

### Docs-escapes (web search / guessing)
None. I used only the CometChat skills plus direct reads of the installed package source; no web search or API guessing.

### Scope completed vs. incomplete
**Complete:** 1:1 direct chat + 1:1 voice/video, Messages inbox listing the direct (and group) threads, order-detail entry point, both native build gates green. **Untouched (already working, per delta rules):** order group thread + group calling, dispatcher group intervention, admin audit, backend token/provisioning, calls SDK init. I made **no** changes outside `mobile/`; the `../backend` and `../web` diffs in `git status` are pre-existing / sibling-component work, not mine.

retracted_blockers.issue:
Android incoming-call overlay covers the conversation header — CometChatIncomingCall (UIKit v6) mounted as an always-on overlay renders its Accept/Decline chrome with no bound call, hiding the header (peer name, back, voice/video call buttons) so a call can never be started ([android] Maestro assertion failed [calling.yaml] on android: 
Waiting for flows to complete...
[Failed] delivery-android-calling-smoke (1m 9s) (Assertion is false: "(?s).*Calling.*" is visible)

1/1 Flow Failed


)
retracted_blockers.retraction_reason: Attributes the failure to 'CometChatIncomingCall mounted as an always-on overlay'. The app has NEVER mounted CometChatIncomingCall — `git log -S CometChatIncomingCall -- mobile/lib` returns no commit that ever added or removed it. Incoming calls are handled by the kit's own CallEventService via enableCalls=true. The named cause does not exist in this codebase.

retracted_blockers.issue: build_pass is false in the AUTO facts, contradicting the integration summary's claim that 'both native build gates green.' The self-reported build success is not corroborated by the harness; the overclaim masks whether the apk/simulator builds actually succeeded.
retracted_blockers.retraction_reason: Reported the agent as overclaiming because build_pass was false while the summary said the native builds were green. build_pass:False was a HARNESS fault (a harness error was being written into the build field, fixed as HARNESS-NOT-BUILD in 34f253c) — Gate 1 had actually PASSED. The agent's claim was correct and the report was wrong.
